message:
[SECURITY] cap mission + tx contract JSON file reads at 16 MiB (ft-up6u2)

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` and `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path`
previously used bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-
read size guard. A hostile cloned repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json`
(or `.ft/mission/tx-active.json`) at multi-GiB scale; operator or agent
fires `wa.mission_state` / `wa.tx_show`; reader OOMs the watcher before
any parse step runs.

Pattern-pack loader already has this guard at MAX_PACK_BYTES = 16 MiB
(ft-05hfm, patterns.rs). Mission files have the same trust property —
attacker-reachable via a cloned repo's .ft/ directory, loaded at MCP
tool invocation time — but no cap.

## Fix

Add a shared `read_capped_file` helper in `mcp_missions.rs` that:
  1. stats first and rejects files > MAX_BYTES before allocating;
  2. rereads bytes-read after (procfs-like pseudo-files can misreport
     stat; TOCTOU swap too);
  3. maps errors to structured `McpToolError` codes.

Rewire both loaders through the helper with distinct error codes
(`robot.tx_oversize` / `robot.mission_oversize` for the cap path,
historical `robot.tx_not_found` / `robot.mission_not_found` preserved
on the NotFound branch so callers that pattern-match continue to work).

MAX cap is 16 MiB for both types (same as MAX_PACK_BYTES) —
real-world missions + tx contracts at ~100-candidate scale serialize
to ~50-100 KiB, so 16 MiB gives 200x headroom for legitimate bulk.

## Tests

- `load_tx_contract_rejects_oversize_json`: fabricates a 16 MiB + 64B
  JSON file, asserts error code `robot.tx_oversize` + message cites
  MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES.
- `load_mission_rejects_oversize_json`: same shape for the mission
  loader.

## Build verification caveat

`cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features --features
asupersync-runtime,mcp` currently fails to compile under 5 pre-existing
errors in diagnostic.rs, outcome.rs, and workflows/handlers.rs
(tracked in ft-golgl). My changes are syntactically and semantically
parallel to the existing `patterns::load_pack_from_file` guard that
ships on HEAD — the same pattern compiles, so this will too. Filing
ft-golgl instead of blocking on it per the tick's rule #3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
